rows.content: This panel displays the current settings of the PF keys and allows
you to change them.  You change a PF key definition by typing over
the current definition.  Primary commands are typed as-is; line
commands are prefixed with a colon (:).
The sample panel above shows the initial settings for your keys,
which give you one-keystroke access to some frequently used general
commands.  The initial settings are all primary commands; you can
also use line and macro commands.The appearance of the panel will
depend on the keyboard. With Enhanced Keyboard, the panel will match
the illustration.  With AT keyboards (40 PF keys), the first panel
will display PF1 - PF10, the second PF11 - PF20, and so on.
The initial settings for keys beyond PF12 repeat PF1-PF12 settings.
You can modify these definitions as desired without affecting the
